'''Sam Powell''' (born 3 July 1992) is an English [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sh}} or {{rlp|hk}} for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]] and the [[England Knights]] at international level.<ref name="England Knights v Jamaica">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/england-knights-v-jamaica-international-2019/719012#lineup|title=England Knights v Jamaica|publisher=WWOS|date=20 October 2019|accessdate=24 October 2019}}</ref>

He has spent time on [[Super League#Dual registration|dual-registration]] from [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] with [[Workington Town]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and with the [[South Wales Ironmen|South Wales Scorpions]] in [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rlcm.com.au/latest-news/21745-rugby-league|title=South Wales Scorpions hail Northern Rail revival			|work=Rugby League Coaching Manuals|date=13 March 2013|accessdate= 7 April 2013}}</ref> After signing for Wigan as a youngster his first team début came in a 2012 Super League game against [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]].<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.wigantoday.net/sport/wigan-warriors/a-dream-debut-for-powell-1-4806956|title=A dream début for Powell|newspaper=Wigan Observer|date= 6 August 2012|accessdate= 7 April 2013}}</ref>

==Background==
Powell was born in [[Wigan]], Greater Manchester, England.

== Club career ==
=== Wigan Warriors ===
Powell signed for the [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] academy aged 16 and progressed through the ranks winning the Academy Grand Final against [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] in 2011.

==== 2012 ====
Powell got his debut for Wigan against [[Hull F.C.]] during August, scoring a try in the 48–10 victory. He started at scrum-half after [[Brett Finch]] suffered a hamstring injury.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors/a-dream-debut-for-powell-1-4806956|title=A dream debut for Powell|website=wigantoday.net|language=en|access-date=2018-01-19}}</ref>

==== 2013 ====
Powell made 16 appearances scoring tries against [[Hull Kingston Rovers|Hull KR]], [[London Broncos]] and [[Huddersfield Giants]]. His kicking game was one of the reasons for his run in the team, a long range drop goal winning the game against [[Widnes Vikings]] in early June. Powell was then called up to the [[England Knights]] squad alongside fellow Warriors [[Dom Crosby|Dominic Crosby]], [[Iain Thornley]] and [[Scott Taylor (rugby league)|Scott Taylor]]. He made his debut for the side against [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] scoring two tries in the 52–16 victory.<ref>{{Cite news|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/eng-knigh-vs-samoa/39744|title=Rugby League World Cup: England Knights thrash Samoa 52-16 at the Salford City Stadium|publisher=Sky Sports|access-date=2018-01-19|language=en}}</ref>

==== 2014 ====
Powell was handed the number 19 shirt for the new season and made 22 appearances scoring against [[Bradford Bulls]], [[Salford Red Devils]] and [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]]. Halfway through the season coach [[Shaun Wane]] decided to experiment with the halfback pairing, moving [[George Williams (rugby league, born 1994)|George Williams]] from hooker to stand off, with Powell starting at dummy half. This would become a permanent switch after Williams impressed in the halves.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors/sam-and-george-trading-places-1-6710766|title=Sam and George trading places|website=wigantoday.net|language=en|access-date=2018-01-19}}</ref>

He was part of the team that lost the [[2014 Super League Grand Final]] to [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]] in a game famous for a [[Ben Flower]] red card.<ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/11156623/St-Helens-14-Wigan-Warriors-6-Moment-of-madness-from-Wales-international-Ben-Flower-costs-Wigan-dear.html|title= St Helens 14 Wigan Warriors 6: Moment of madness from Wales international Ben Flower costs Wigan dear|date= 11 October 2014 |work=The Daily Telegraph|accessdate=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/oct/11/st-helens-wigan-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title= St Helens win Grand Final after Wigan's Ben Flower is sent off|date= 11 October 2014 |work=The Guardian |accessdate=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/29559845|title= St Helens 14–6 Wigan Warriors|date= 11 October 2014 |publisher=BBC Sport|accessdate=15 October 2014}}</ref>

==== 2015 ====
The new Super League season play 23 times for the Wigan club with more appearances coming from the bench showing a positional change from halfback to hooker. His only try of the season came against [[Hull F.C.]] in late July.

He made his second appearance at [[Old Trafford]] losing out to the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in a tense [[2015 Super League Grand Final]].<ref name="Leeds pip Wigan to seal treble after brilliant, breathless Grand Final">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/oct/10/leeds-wigan-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title=Leeds pip Wigan to seal treble after brilliant, breathless Grand Final|work=The Guardian|date=10 October 2015|accessdate=8 November 2019}}</ref>

==== 2016 ====
Powell was handed the number 16 shirt for the new season, an early indication that he would be playing mainly from the bench replacing [[Michael McIlorum]] at hooker. This however was not to be the case after McIlorum suffered a fractured and dislocated ankle against the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in just the third game of the season.<ref>{{Cite news|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12215/10176171/wigan-fear-michael-mcilorum-could-be-out-for-six-months|title=Wigan fear Michael McIlorum could be out for six months|publisher=Sky Sports|access-date=2018-01-19|language=en}}</ref> Despite initial speculation that Wigan would have to bring in a replacement Powell made the role his own playing 36 times playing on many occasions 80 minutes per game. Tries again [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]], [[Castleford Tigers]], [[Wakefield Trinity]] and [[Hull F.C.]] ensured it was his most successful season in both appearances and scores.

He won the [[Super League Grand Final]] on the third time of asking in a tense affair which saw the Wigan Warriors beat the [[Warrington Wolves]] 12–6 at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2016/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-warrington-wigan-live|title=Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!|work=The Guardian|date=8 October 2016|accessdate=6 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37550948|title=Super League Grand Final: Warrington Wolves 6-12 Wigan Warriors|date=2016-10-08|publisher=BBC Sport|access-date=2018-01-19|language=en-GB}}</ref>

==== 2017 ====
With McIlorum still out injured, Powell started the season at hooker and played his 100th game for the Wigan club in the 22-6 [[World Club Challenge]] victory over the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors/powell-prepares-for-a-special-milestone-1-8393363|title=Powell prepares for a special milestone|website=wigantoday.net|language=en|access-date=2018-01-19}}</ref> McIlorum returned in the Good Friday derby against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] but Powell kept his place in the team making 31 appearances in all competitions scoring twice and kicking 6 goals.

He played in the [[2017 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-final-live-score-11054626|title=Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs|work=Mirror|date=26 August 2017|accessdate=6 November 2019}}</ref>

==== 2018 ====
The departure of [[Michael McIlorum|McIlorum]] meant that [[Thomas Leuluai]] was handed the number 9 shirt with Powell moving to the number 7.

He played in the [[2018 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2018/oct/13/super-league-grand-final-2018-wigan-warriors-v-warrington-wolves-live?page=with:block-5bc1ff43e4b05798be94cc63#liveblog-navigation|title=Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened|work=The Guardian|date=13 October 2018|accessdate=5 November 2019}}</ref>

====2019====
Powell played 25 games for Wigan in 2019 as they suffered a shock semi-final loss against Salford.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/wigan-vs-salford/52328/|title=Salford seal maiden Super League Grand Final appearance|publisher=Sky Sports}}</ref>

====2020====
Powell played in the [[2020 Super League Grand Final]] which Wigan lost 8–4 against St Helens.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55072997|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan 4-8 St Helens|publisher=BBC}}</ref>

====2021, 2022 & 2023====
Powell played 23 matches for Wigan in the 2021 Super League season as the club reached the playoffs but were eliminated by Leeds. The following season, Powell played in Wigan's upset semi-final loss against Leeds scoring the only try of the match. On 14 October 2023, Powell played in Wigan's [[2023 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/oct/14/catalans-dragons-wigan-super-league-grand-final-rugby-league-match-report/|title=Wigan Warriors crowned Super League champions after downing Dragons|website=The Guardian}}</ref>
On 10 November 2023 it was reported that he had signed for [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] in the [[Super League]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|publisher=Love Rugby League|date=10 November 2023|title=Sam Powell: Warrington Wolves move confirmed with Wigan Warriors hooker becoming Sam Burgess' eighth addition|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/sam-powell-warrington-wolves-move-confirmed-with-wigan-warriors-hooker-becoming-sam-burgess-eighth-addition}}</ref>

===2024===
On 8 June, Powell played in Warrington's [[2024 Challenge Cup final]] defeat against Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c2jj1x7m71zo|title=Wigan beat Warrington to win Challenge Cup|publisher=BBC}}</ref>

===2025===
On 7 June, Powell played in Warrington's 8-6 Challenge Cup final loss against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn0gnqllpxlo|title=In pictures: Hull KR Challenge Cup victory parade|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

==International career==
In July 2018 he was selected in the [[England Knights]] Performance squad.<ref name="Ten new additions in England Knights squad">{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ten-new-additions-in-england-knights-squad/|title=Ten new additions in England Knights squad|publisher=loverugbyleague|date=24 July 2018|accessdate=31 October 2019}}</ref> Later that year he was selected for the England Knights on their tour of [[Papua New Guinea]]. He played against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] at the Lae Football Stadium<ref name="Papua New Guinea vs England Knights">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/papua-new-guinea-v-england-knights-international-2018/718006#lineup|title=Papua New Guinea vs England Knights|publisher=WWOS|date=27 October 2018|accessdate=30 October 2019}}</ref> and the Oil Search National Football Stadium.<ref name="Papua New Guinea v England Knights">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/papua-new-guinea-v-england-knights-international-2018/718007#lineup|title=Papua New Guinea v England Knights|publisher=WWOS|date=3 November 2018|accessdate=30 October 2019}}</ref>

In 2019 he was selected for the England Knights against [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium]].<ref name="England Knights vs Jamaica">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/england-knights-v-jamaica-international-2019/719012#lineup|title=England Knights vs Jamaica|publisher=WWOS|date=20 October 2019|accessdate=30 October 2019}}</ref>
